headline: White House recalibrates Anthropic strategy amid Mythos threat
subtitle: Government softens AI feud · Gemini enters vehicles · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: After months of escalating tensions with Anthropic, the White House is reconsidering its hard line now that Mythos has demonstrated advanced cyber capabilities. A new national security memo aims to thread the needle between accessing powerful AI models while maintaining public pressure.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access
bullets.summary: The administration is opposing Anthropic's proposal to increase private sector access to Mythos from roughly 50 to 120 companies, citing concerns about government compute availability. An upcoming national security memo will address some Pentagon grievances while allowing agencies to bypass supply chain restrictions.

bullets.text: Google deploys Gemini across vehicle infotainment systems
bullets.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini in cars with Google built-in, replacing Assistant with conversational AI for navigation, climate control, and vehicle diagnostics. General Motors will add the feature to approximately 4 million vehicles starting with 2022 model years.

bullets.text: Perplexity Deep Research helps validate business concepts
bullets.summary: Users can leverage Perplexity's Deep Research mode to systematically stress-test business ideas by running a reusable prompt that generates comprehensive market analysis and feasibility reports in minutes.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's creature fixation to single reward signal
bullets.summary: Investigation revealed ChatGPT's obsession with goblins and gremlins originated from the Nerdy personality preset, where creature mentions spiked 175% after ChatGPT-5.1 launch. OpenAI retired the problematic preset and deployed goblin-blocking prompts in GPT-5.5.

bullets.text: Stripe launches AI agent wallet with human transaction approval
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced a new wallet system enabling AI agents to make purchases while requiring human approval on every transaction, bridging autonomous AI capability with necessary oversight.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security for enterprise vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Claude Security beta allows enterprises to deploy Opus 4.7 for automated codebase vulnerability detection and patch generation, providing autonomous security assessment capabilities.

bullets.text: Cloudflare lets agents provision accounts, buy domains, deploy
bullets.summary: Agents can now complete account setup, subscription activation, domain registration and deployment autonomously while humans maintain final approval over permissions and terms. This removes tedious administrative overhead and makes infrastructure accessible to non-technical users.

bullets.text: Stripe releases agent-focused payments and commerce tools
bullets.summary: Stripe announced an Agentic Commerce Suite with Link wallet support for agents and streaming payments. The new Link CLI provides one-time credentials for agents without exposing actual payment details, enabling safer autonomous transactions.

bullets.text: Cursor SDK enables building agents with Cursor's runtime
bullets.summary: Developers can now build agents using Cursor's same runtime, harness and models. Applications range from CI/CD pipeline automation to embedding agents directly into products and workflows.

bullets.text: Warp open-sourced with OpenAI as founding sponsor
bullets.summary: Terminal tool Warp is now open-source with OpenAI backing the repo. The strategy leverages human contributors for design direction while agents handle implementation work at scale.

bullets.text: Lightfield ships AI-native CRM with plain English workflows
bullets.summary: New AI-native CRM now includes Skills feature allowing users to define workflows in natural language. Agents execute against full data graphs with code execution, web search and file I/O capabilities.

main_story.title: White House rethinks adversarial stance toward Anthropic
main_story.body: Months of Pentagon pressure against Anthropic has collided with geopolitical reality. The emergence of Mythos with sophisticated cyber capabilities has forced the White House to abandon its hardline approach and pursue a more pragmatic accommodation. A forthcoming national security memo will ostensibly address some of the department's concerns while allowing federal agencies to acquire Mythos access despite ongoing legal disputes. The administration is blocking Anthropic's plan to dramatically expand private sector access to Mythos, citing compute constraints that could impact government priorities. However, internal division persists—military officials continue attacking Anthropic's leadership while the broader security apparatus pushes for closer collaboration.
main_story.why: Mythos's advanced capabilities make it strategically valuable for national security applications, forcing the government to recalibrate from confrontation to controlled cooperation. Balancing competition concerns against national security imperatives requires nuanced policy rather than the Pentagon's earlier scorched-earth approach.
main_story.take: The White House faces an uncomfortable truth: technological leadership demands engaging with companies you might distrust. Maintaining an open adversarial posture while trying to monopolize access to breakthrough AI reveals the fundamental tension between ideology and pragmatism in tech policy.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI exceeded its 2029 Stargate compute goal, securing 10 GW capacity
Elon Musk revealed xAI used distillation techniques to train on OpenAI models
Meta opened its ads platform to third-party AI agents via new MCP server
Cursor released Security Review for automated vulnerability detection and patching
